title: Fika Jobs raises $4M to build a video-first hiring platform where AI agents interview candidates
slides: Could AI agents replace human recruiters entirely?
Fika Jobs just raised $4 million for video interviews.
These AI agents analyze tone and micro-expressions live.
You now hire without ever watching a single video.
Test one AI interview on your next open role today.
thread_text:
1. AI replaces your hiring gut?

Fika Jobs just raised $4 million to let AI agents analyze tone and micro-expressions live during interviews.

2. No video needed.

The AI makes a hire decision without you ever watching a single recording of the candidate.

3. What gets lost?

Micro-expressions are read by models trained on biased data—candidates who mask emotions get flagged as "low fit."

4. The recruiter becomes a bystander.

Your role shrinks to clicking "approve" on a recommendation generated by black-box analysis.

5. Test one on your next open role today.

Run a sample interview through Fika's tool—then compare the AI's verdict with your own impression. Does it match?
video_script: Fika Jobs just got four million dollars.
Their AI reads your face live.
It checks tone and tiny expressions.
You never even watch the video.
Try one AI interview this week.

script: Fika Jobs just got four million dollars.
Their AI reads your face live.
It checks tone and tiny expressions.
You never even watch the video.
Try one AI interview this week.

instagram.caption:
An AI agent just analyzed your facial tics during a job interview and decided you're not a fit—without a human watching any of it.  

Fika Jobs raised $4 million to build a hiring platform where AI agents conduct live video interviews and analyze tone plus micro-expressions in real time. The recruiter never sees a second of footage; they just approve or reject the AI's verdict.  

Your next job application will be judged by a model trained on biased data, not by a person. 💼🤖  

Would you trust an AI agent to assess your candidacy based on how you blink?

carousel_slides.description: Fika Jobs raised $4M for AI analyzing tone and micro-expressions live during interviews.
The AI makes a hire decision without you ever watching a single recording of the candidate.
Models trained on biased data flag candidates who mask emotions as 'low fit'—fairness questioned.
Recruiters become bystanders clicking approve on black-box recommendations from AI.

carousel_slides.hook: Test this AI on your next hire.
